heading: Key Takeaway
text: Motion is a change of position; it can be described in terms of the distance moved or the displacement.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: The motion of an object could be uniform or non-uniform depending on whether its velocity is constant or changing.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: The speed of an object is the distance covered per unit time, and velocity is the displacement per unit time.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: The acceleration of an object is the change in velocity per unit time.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Uniform and non-uniform motions of objects can be shown through graphs.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: The motion of an object moving at uniform acceleration can be described with the help of the following equations, namely v = u + at s = ut + ½ at2 2as = v2 – u2 where u is initial velocity of the object, which moves with uniform acceleration a for time t, v is its final velocity and s is the distance it travelled in time t.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: If an object moves in a circular path with uniform speed, its motion is called uniform circular motion.
